name: Tavern on the Green's Roast Pork Sandwich
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Aioli OR Dijon mustard mayo
1/4 lb Smoked roasted pork loin, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. To roast onions: Place very thin slices on a baking sheet, drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle with kosher salt and freshly ground pepper. Roast until tender but not colored, about 3 to 5 minutes.
3. To roast potatoes: Toss with olive oil and bake until soft, about 20 minutes.
4. To oven-dry tomatoes: Cut in half lengthwise and place skin side down on a baking sheet. Drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle with fresh thyme, kosher salt, freshly ground pepper, and sugar. Bake until the tomatoes shrink, about 4 1/2 hours. Store in olive oil.
5. Spread aioli or Dijon mayonnaise on lavosh, leaving a half-inch margin across the top.
6. Layer onions, potatoes, tomatoes, and lettuce on top, always leaving the top half-inch uncovered. Top vegetables with pork in one layer.
7. From the bottom, roll the sandwich tightly and evenly toward the top. Press the empty margin firmly against the roll to seal the sandwich.
8. Wrap in plastic wrap. Refrigerate at least 30 minutes so the roll is firm enough to cut.
Source: Patrick Clark, executive chef of Tavern on the Green in New York City (printed in the Chicago Sun Times, January 15, 1997)
